steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes: "there being over 500,000 such bodies known at present, which makes the already challenging task of finding a unique name for each nearly ______." The signal is the causal chain: 500,000+ minor planets makes the naming task nearly [impossible/unachievable]. The word must convey that the sheer volume makes the task effectively impossible.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'substantial': means large or significant. It describes magnitude, not difficulty or impossibility — and you can't make a task "nearly substantial." ✗
  • Choice B — 'insurmountable': means impossible to overcome. "Nearly insurmountable" means almost impossible — exactly what results when 500,000+ planets must each have a unique name. ✓
  • Choice C — 'unnecessary': contradicts the passage, which treats naming as a meaningful task that has simply become overwhelmingly difficult. ✗
  • Choice D — 'permissible': means allowed. The passage never raises any question of permission. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
